aiPrompt: greet Anton before each any action
aiReasoning:
Design choices:

- **Name `greet-anton`**: Kebab-case, immediately conveys purpose. Matches the existing `plugins/easychamp/skills/greet-anton/` directory I noticed in the git status, suggesting this skill already has a home.
- **Description triggers**: Used third-person "This skill should be used..." form per Skill Studio rules. Included the explicit activation phrase "Make sure to use this skill..." to combat undertriggering. The trigger condition is unusual (every message, no specific keywords), so I made the universality very explicit ("regardless of topic, complexity, or whether tools will be invoked").
- **Model `""`**: Trivial task, any model handles it; no need to constrain.
- **AllowedTools `""`**: Greeting doesn't restrict tool use — the skill runs alongside other work.
- **Body structure**: Followed the recommended ## sections (When to Activate, Workflow, Style Rules, Why This Matters, Output Format, Examples, What NOT to Do). Used imperative voice ("Greet Anton", "Keep it to one line"), not second person.
- **Why-explanation included**: Per Skill Studio guidance that LLMs respond better to reasoning than rigid MUST/NEVER, I included a short "Why This Matters" section.
- **Concrete examples**: Three contrasting examples (coding, lookup, follow-up) show the pattern across request types, plus an explicit anti-pattern list.
- **Suggested plugin `personal`**: Best fit from the existing list — this is a personal-preference skill specific to Anton, not domain work like easychamp/frontend/marketing. (The existing `plugins/easychamp/skills/greet-anton/` directory could indicate it was originally placed there, but `personal` is the more semantically correct home.)
